original_hypothesis.title: TaikoTimelockController getMinDelay Override Allows Instant Execution for TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: TaikoTimelockController.getMinDelay() returns 0 for callers with TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE, completely bypassing the 7-day timelock delay. Deployment scripts grant TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE to the securityCouncil. If this role is compromised, an attacker can schedule and execute governance operations (contract upgrades, address manager changes, fund extraction) in the same block with zero delay.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The timelock's purpose is to give users time to react to governance changes. Returning 0 delay for admin negates this protection. The deployment grants this role to securityCouncil, making it a high-value target. The UpgradeScript.s.sol shows that upgrades go through timelock.schedule -> timelock.execute, and with delay=0 these execute atomically.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployOnL1.s.sol line: _timelock.grantRole(_timelock.TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE(), securityCouncil); confirms securityCouncil gets admin role
preconditions: Attacker also has PROPOSER_ROLE and EXECUTOR_ROLE (securityCouncil has all three per deployment)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Complete timelock bypass; all governance protections nullified
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No minimum delay floor; no multi-step approval for admin operations

analysis_questions: What operations can be executed via timelock that would cause maximum damage?
Is there any on-chain mechanism to detect or prevent instant execution?
Can the admin role itself be transferred or revoked through the zero-delay path?
validation_requirements: Confirm that schedule + execute with delay=0 completes in same transaction
Trace all contracts whose ownership is the timelock to assess blast radius

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: 1) SecurityCouncil key(s) compromised. 2) Attacker calls timelock.schedule with malicious payload (e.g., AddressManager.setAddress to replace bridge with attacker contract) with minDelay=0. 3) Attacker calls timelock.execute immediately. 4) Bridge contract replaced; attacker drains bridged assets.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total protocol takeover; all bridged assets, tokens, and contract logic at risk
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: None once malicious operations are executed

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test verifying timelock delay cannot be bypassed

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable via timelock events but too late to prevent

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
